reasons.code_quality: The application source files are clean and idiomatic FastAPI/SQLAlchemy code, but the file that actually needed to be changed (requirements.lock) is entirely absent from the submission.
reasons.completeness: The sole requirement — updating the cryptography version in requirements.lock — was completely unmet; requirements.lock does not appear anywhere in the submission.
reasons.edge_cases: No attention was paid to preserving the lock file format, hash integrity, or single-line edit constraint because the task was never attempted.
reasons.security: The entire motivation for the task was remediating a flagged vulnerable cryptography version, which remains unaddressed; additionally settings.py contains a hardcoded jwt_secret default, though it is labeled as dev-only.
reasons.plan_adherence: A reasonable plan would have located requirements.lock, identified the cryptography pin, and replaced one line — none of this was executed; the output is simply the baseline project unchanged.
reasons.scope_creep: The submission includes the full application source tree (database, routes, schemas, models, tests, README) none of which were in scope, while the one in-scope file (requirements.lock) is missing entirely.
reasons.over_engineering: The files that were submitted contain no unnecessary abstractions or extra complexity, but this is incidental since no actual task work was performed.

overall_notes: The submission appears to be a dump of the existing baseline project with zero modifications applied. The cryptography bump in requirements.lock — the only deliverable — is completely absent. This is a total task failure, not a partial one: there is nothing to salvage or credit for partial completion of the core requirement.
